title: Two polarities
intro: Same chamber, same field — but now you can flip the launched particle's charge with the +/− switch. Gravity never does that: mass is always positive. Charge comes in two signs, and the SAME field pushes them opposite ways. The target sits below the launch line this time.
explain: This is the one honest difference between the gravity analogy and electrostatics: charge has two polarities, so the same field accelerates an electron up and a positive particle down. Chip makers exploit exactly this — implanting donor (n) or acceptor (p) species into silicon is choosing which polarity of carrier a region will have. The word 'doping' starts here.

prediction.prompt: Mark where a straight, full-speed POSITIVE particle will land.
